name: La Marensine Cycling Loop
name_original: La Marensine — boucle au départ de Seignosse
description: Cycle the moderate 47.0-mile La Marensine loop from Seignosse, exploring pine forests, beaches, and the Étang Noir Nature Reserve.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Seignosse features an extensive network of dedicated cycle paths, including a significant section of the Vélodyssée coastal greenway. The landscape is characterized by vast pine forests, sandy beaches along the Atlantic coast, and scenic freshwater lakes such as Étang Blanc and Étang Noir. Routes often traverse a mix of paved surfaces, offering varied terrain for cyclists. This region provides a diverse natural environment for exploring on two wheels. Best touring cycling routes around Seignosse The most popular touring cycling route is Seignosse: Étang Blanc and Étang Noir Loop, a 13.2 miles (21.2 km) moderate trail that takes about 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural beauty around the Étang Blanc and Étang Noir lakes, often passing through shaded pine forests. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Marensine Cycling Loop, a moderate 47.0 miles (75.6 km) path. This route guides riders through pine forests, past beaches, and near the Étang Noir Nature Reserve, showcasing the diverse coastal and forest scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Cork oak avenue – The Dunes Near Hossegor loop from Seignosse, a 26.1 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through cork oak avenues and near the dunes of Hossegor, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Seignosse is defined by extensive pine forests, Atlantic beaches, and serene lakes, with dedicated cycle paths like the Vélodyssée. The network offers options for easy family rides as well as more demanding routes. The routes in Seignosse are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 2700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Seignosse's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Seignosse?
A: There are over 75 touring cycling routes around Seignosse, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy, family-friendly rides to more demanding routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Seignosse?
A: The routes around Seignosse feature a diverse mix of terrain. You'll primarily find mostly paved surfaces, making them suitable for various skill levels. Many paths wind through extensive pine forests, along sandy beaches, and past scenic freshwater lakes like Étang Blanc and Étang Noir.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Seignosse?
A: Yes, Seignosse offers several easy routes perfect for families. The region's dedicated cycle paths, including sections of the Vélodyssée, are protected from road traffic, ensuring a safe and pleasant experience for cyclists of all ages. Many routes are designed to be accessible and enjoyable for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling in Seignosse allows you to explore a rich natural environment. You can ride through fragrant pine forests, enjoy views of the Atlantic Ocean from sandy beaches, and discover serene lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hossegor Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6068030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang Noir Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights include the Port of Capbreton and charming villages of Les Landes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Seignosse?
A: Yes, many routes in Seignosse are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41509856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Capbreton – Capbreton Marina loop from Le Penon&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic ride around the harbor and marina.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Seignosse?
A: The touring cycling routes in Seignosse are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive network of dedicated cycle paths, the beautiful natural landscapes including pine forests and lakes, and the overall welcoming environment for exploring on two wheels.

Q: Where can I find bike rental services or assistance for touring cyclists in Seignosse?
A: Seignosse is well-equipped for cyclists, offering "BIKE RECEPTION" certified services. These services provide advice, assistance, equipment fixing, and water. For more information on cycle paths and bike rental options, you can visit the local tourism website: &lt;a href="https://www.seignosse-tourisme.com/en/things-to-do-in-seignosse/hikes/cycle-paths-bike-rental/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;seignosse-tourisme.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous Vélodyssée coastal greenway?
A: Absolutely. Seignosse features a significant section of the Vélodyssée, a major coastal greenway that traverses the town from north to south. Many local touring cycling routes integrate with or run parallel to this protected path, offering a fantastic long-distance cycling experience.

Q: Can I find places to eat or shop for supplies along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes pass through areas where you can find amenities. For instance, the Zone Pédebert is a popular area with surfwear factory outlets and small restaurants, providing convenient stops for refreshments and supplies during your ride.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Seignosse?
A: Seignosse offers a range of difficulty levels. While most routes are considered easy to moderate, with 60 easy and 11 moderate tours, there are also 6 more challenging routes available. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced touring cyclists can find suitable options.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the ocean or beaches?
A: Many routes in Seignosse provide glimpses of the Atlantic Ocean and run alongside beautiful beaches like Bourdaines. The region's extensive coastline means you can often enjoy ocean views and experience stunning sunrises and sunsets over the Atlantic while cycling.

Q: Which route explores the cork oak avenues and dunes near Hossegor?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41046629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cork oak avenue – The Dunes Near Hossegor loop from Seignosse&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This route guides you through picturesque cork oak avenues and offers views near the famous dunes of Hossegor, providing a unique blend of forest and coastal scenery.
tips: Protecting 52 ha of wetlands, the Nature Reserve is home to characteristic habitats of the Landes accessible by a slatted path: swamp forest, peat bog, pond, stream. The Black Pond which gives its name to the reserve is distinguished by its deep waters and muddy bottoms.
The path is a little sandy, but it is worth to access the forests and deserted beaches

This chapel dates from the 13th century. It is all that remains of the Benedictine priory of Gourby which owes its origin to the Benedictines of Cagnotte. Cagnotte Abbey itself was founded in all probability around the year 900…
Source: https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Église_Saint-Blaise_de_Gourby

In Saubusse, you follow the Adour on the old towpath which has become EuroVelo n°3 the Scandibérique. The Barthes, vast flooded meadows shared by geese, horses and cows, not forgetting egrets, herons, storks as well as a rich flora of wet meadows.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the La Marensine loop from Seignosse?
faqs.answer: The 75.6 km loop is designed for touring bicycles and generally takes around 4 hours and 22 minutes to complete. This duration is for continuous cycling and does not include stops for sightseeing or breaks.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Marensine loop?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring bikes. It incorporates sections of the Vélodyssée greenway and an old towpath along the Adour river, which is now part of the EuroVelo n°3. While mostly paved, some segments in the region may be unpaved, though the overall difficulty is moderate.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or children?
faqs.answer: Classified as a 'moderate' bike ride, the La Marensine loop requires a good fitness level. While not technically challenging, its length of 75.6 km might be demanding for beginners or very young children. Families with older, experienced cycling children might find it enjoyable, especially if planning stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Marensine loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the La Marensine loop are not provided, Seignosse is known for its extensive cycling infrastructure. A common starting point for routes in the area is often near the Hall des Sports in Seignosse Bourg, where parking is typically available.

faqs.question: What are the notable sights or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers diverse scenery and passes several points of interest. You'll cycle near the picturesque Étang Noir Nature Reserve, the Dunes des Casernes, and the Saint Blaise Chapel, Gourby. The route also goes through the renowned Hossegor golf course and offers glimpses of beautiful Basque-Landes-style architecture.

faqs.question: Is the La Marensine loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is a public cycling route, so dogs are generally permitted, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific rules for nature reserves like the Étang Noir Nature Reserve should be checked locally, as restrictions might apply to protect wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Landes region, where the La Marensine loop is located, is pleasant for cycling from spring through early autumn. These seasons offer mild temperatures and less chance of extreme weather, making for a more comfortable ride. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable then.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the La Marensine loop. It utilizes public cycling paths and sections of national and European routes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Marensine loop is well-integrated into the regional cycling network. It incorporates sections of the Vélodyssée and the EuroVelo n°3 (Scandibérique). You'll also find it intersects with local paths like Piste Cyclable - Léon Seignosse and Liaison vélo : Seignosse - Soorts - Hossegor.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the La Marensine loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several settlements, including Saubusse, Seignosse-le-Penon, Azur, Magescq, and Hossegor. These areas offer opportunities for dining, cafes, and shops where you can refuel and take a break.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse landscape, transitioning between natural beauty and charming urban areas. The route takes you through maritime pine forests, offers glimpses of the ocean and sandy beaches, and skirts the Étang Noir Nature Reserve with its lush vegetation. You'll also see the manicured Hossegor golf course and traditional Basque-Landes-style architecture.
